title: Enterolobium guaraniticum
intro: Enterolobium guaraniticum, also known as the "Tatú" or "Palo de tatu", is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The health benefits of Enterolobium guaraniticum include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Enterolobium guaraniticum is commonly prepared as a tea or infusión, and also used in the production of tinctures and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Enterolobium guaraniticum is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is part of the genus Enterolobium, which consists of tropical trees native to South America. The plant's classification is further defined by its subfamily Caesalpinioideae and tribe Caesalpinieae. As a member of the Caesalpinioideae subfamily, it shares characteristics with other legume trees. This classification highlights the plant's unique position among other tropical tree species.
